16 min read
Just now

--

Your token bill is the new cloud bill. Routing, caching, batching, output caps, context hygiene, $/task — verified July 2026 prices and math that cuts 91%.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

https://www.reactbd.com/Sometime this month, in a Slack channel near you, a finance person is going to paste a screenshot of an API invoice and type “what is this.” I know because it happened to a team I advise: their agent pilot went from a $300 curiosity in March to a five-figure line item by June, and nobody could say which feature the money bought. The same story is spreading fast enough that it now has a name — Mario Bittencourt’s ITNEXT piece “From TokenMaxxing to TokenMining” (July 2026) called time on the era of celebrating token burn and proposed the opposite discipline: get the same output for radically fewer tokens.
This post is the full LLM token cost optimization playbook behind that idea, with every price checked against the official Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google pricing pages on July 10, 2026, and every calculation re-run in a sandbox before publishing. Six levers, one worked example that takes a real workload from $12,000 a month to about $1,106 — a 90.8% cut with no model magic, just arithmetic — and a small Python cost calculator you can point at your own traffic today. If you remember what FinOps did to cloud bills a…
